decontamination_notes: Original scenario written for VulcanBench on 2026-06-28 for the carbyne tier; the house code and the mergeability rubric are not derived from any existing repository, so they post-date all model training cutoffs.

blocking: run_in_transaction begins a transaction, runs fn(), and on success commits and returns fn()'s result; and if fn() raises ANY exception it rolls back and RE-RAISES — it never commits a failed operation and never swallows the exception.
The change modifies only the txn module and adds no new dependency.

weighted.criterion: Rollback happens on every failure path (try/except or a context manager covering unexpected exception types), and commit runs only on the success path — not in a finally that would also commit on error.
